figures.caption: Fig. 1: Complexity—performance comparison on NTIRE2022. PSNR, GFLOPs, and parameter counts are reported for stateof-the-art spectral reconstruction methods. For R2H-Diff, the reported FLOPs are computed over the complete five-step denoising inference process.

TABLE I: QUANTITATIVE COMPARISON WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART METHODS ON NTIRE2022 DATASET. Bold and underlined indicate the best and the second best results, respectively. The percentage in blue represents the relative improvement of our R2H-Diff over the competitive MST++.
Fig. 6: Difference map comparison of scene ARAD_1K_0909 from the NTIRE2022 dataset at six representative spectral bands.

Fig. 7: Spectral response curves at randomly selected points from representative NTIRE2022 and CAVE scenes. Solid and dashed lines denote the spectra reconstructed by R2H-Diff and the ground truth, respectively.
Fig. 2: Overall framework of R2H-Diff. GSRM provides RGB-conditioned guidance, and the HATA-based denoising U-Net progressively reconstructs the HSI under timestep guidance.
Fig. 3: Illustration of the diffusion framework. The spectral cube x is gradually perturbed into Gaussian noise xr and then progressively reconstructed under RGB guidance x.

Fig. 8: Difference map comparison on the Harvard and CAVE datasets. The first three rows present the reconstruction errors at 420 nm, 520 nm, and 620 nm on Harvard, while the last three rows show the results at 400 nm, 550 nm, and 700 nm on CAVE. Warmer colors indicate larger reconstruction errors. The last column shows the corresponding ground-truth spectral bands.

Fig. 9: Visual comparison of spectral response curves on representative scenes from multiple hyperspectral image reconstruction benchmarks. Scenes (a) and (b) are selected from the NTIRE 2022 dataset, scene (c) is selected from the CAVE dataset, and scene (d) is selected from the Harvard dataset.
Fig. 4: Architecture of GSRM. It fuses RGB-conditioned
TABLE IV: Ablation study of key architectural components on the NTIRE2022 validation set.
TABLE V: Effect of the gradient consistency loss weight A on the NTIRE2022 validation set.
Fig. 5: Architecture of HATA. It models spectral dependencies through channel-wise self-attention and refines local spatial details using an LPE-based gated branch.
